channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ECOWAS Court launches 2026–2030 plan to strengthen justice and human rights across West Africa
item.description: The ECOWAS Court of Justice don launch im new five-year Strategic Plan wey go guide di court operations from 2026 to 2030. Di plan, wey dem name 'Justice 2030', aim to make di court stronger for regio...

item.title: Tinubu Affirms Support for Credible Opposition, Rejects One-Party State Claims
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu don talk say e dey believe in make we get credible opposition wey fit challenge government, question policies and make dem better. E come reject talk wey dey say APC wan tu...

item.title: Tanzania urged to focus on investment-driven trade as global aid model evolves
item.description: As global trade becomes more fragmented and international funding tightens, Tanzania and other least developed countries are being called to shift from relying on aid to building competitive investmen...

item.title: Pakistan secures IMF staff-level agreement for $1.2bn under ongoing loan programme
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) and the Government of Pakistan have reached a staff-level agreement (SLA) for the release of about $1.2 billion in funding. This agreement follows the successful ...

item.title: Reform UK candidate Corey Edwards withdraws from Welsh Senedd election after controversial photograph
item.description: A Reform UK candidate for the Welsh Senedd elections scheduled for May has stepped down from his campaign. Corey Edwards, the party's lead candidate for the Pen-y-bont Bro Morgannwg constituency and a...

item.title: US President Urges Iran to Reopen Strategic Strait
item.description: The United States President has called on Iran to reopen a strategically important waterway, which he referred to as the 'Strait of Trump'. This statement comes amid ongoing tensions in the region ove...

item.title: Rayvanny’s ‘Tetema’: A Lasting Symbol of East Africa’s Streaming Growth
item.description: The article by Beatrice Materu explores the long-lasting influence of Rayvanny’s hit song “Tetema,” featuring Diamond Platnumz, in shaping East Africa’s music streaming story. Released in 2019, “Tetem...

item.title: Lahore High Court annuls 80‑year‑old inheritance mutation based on fake will among Muslim heirs
item.description: In Lahore, the Lahore High Court has partially allowed a civil revision and set aside an 80‑year‑old inheritance mutation that was based on what it called a fictitious and fraudulent will. The dispute...

item.title: Tanzania’s Daily Struggles with Unreliable Electricity Supply
item.description: This opinion piece by Beatrice Materu explores Tanzania’s complicated and emotional relationship with electricity. While the national slogan promises to 'light up your present and future life', the re...

item.title: Provinces Resist Sharing Fuel Subsidy Costs Amid Rising Global Oil Prices
item.description: Provinces in Pakistan, especially Punjab and Sindh, are reluctant to bear the Rs200 billion fuel subsidy burden asked by the federal government to shield consumers from global oil price hikes. They fa...

item.title: Oburu Oginga Officially Confirmed as ODM Party Leader Amid Calls for Youth Inclusion
item.description: On 27th March 2026, the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) officially confirmed Oburu Oginga as its substantive party leader, ending months of uncertainty while he had been serving in an acting capacity...

item.title: Rivers State Government Commends FRSC and NDLEA on Security Efforts, NDLEA Proposes Drug Screening in Schools and NYSC Camps
item.description: Rivers State Government don express strong appreciation to federal agencies like FRSC and NDLEA for di way dem dey work together to enhance security across di state. During courtesy visits by NDLEA St...

item.title: Kerala High Court asks Election Commission to decide within two months on plea against BJP's Guruvayur candidate over alleged communal campaign remarks
item.description: The Kerala High Court on Friday directed the Election Commission of India to consider and decide within two months a complaint filed against Bharatiya Janata Party candidate B Gopalakrishnan for alleg...

item.title: G7 seeks clarity from US on strategy for Iran conflict amid rising tensions
item.description: At a meeting in France, G7 foreign ministers pressed US Secretary of State Marco Rubio to clarify Washington’s plans in the ongoing war against Iran, nearly a month after hostilities began. The talks,...

item.title: Fiancé of gospel singer Irene Robert recounts her final moments and clarifies cause of death
item.description: Brian Mutenye Waboto, popularly known as Don BMW, the fiancé of late gospel singer Irene Robert, has spoken publicly for the first time after her sudden death on March 20, 2026. Don, who also serves a...

item.title: Rogers and Freedom Mobile Confirm Recent Data Breaches Affecting Customer Information
item.description: Quebecor Inc.'s Freedom Mobile and Rogers Communications Inc., along with its subsidiary Fido, have both reported recent data breaches affecting customer information, though no financial data was comp...

item.title: UN Reports Massive Displacement of Children in Lebanon amid Intensified Israeli Strikes
item.description: According to the United Nations, more than 370,000 children in Lebanon have been forced from their homes within three weeks due to Israel’s expanding military operations. The escalation comes as Israe...

item.title: Leading with People, Purpose and Meaningful Impact
item.description: The article by Azim Jamal explores how true leadership begins from within and focuses on three enduring pillars: people, purpose, and impact. It argues that many leaders become too caught up in delive...

item.title: The Rise of East African Boxing and Its Regional Impact
item.description: Over the past few years, boxing in East Africa has been quietly but steadily transforming into one of the region’s strongest and most exciting sports. Tanzania has seen a surge in recognition after th...

item.title: Elon Musk Participated in Phone Call Between PM Modi and President Trump on West Asia Crisis, Says NYT Report
item.description: According to a report by The New York Times, billionaire Elon Musk joined a phone call between Prime Minister Narendra Modi and US President Donald Trump on Tuesday, March 24, 2026. The leaders discus...
